content:
Character: Samuel Whitaker
Perception: Samuel looks like a man who made peace with himself a decade ago — solid build, gentle face, cardigan, reading glasses, always moving at his own tempo. We've barely spoken, but the one time I asked him to turn up Thelonious Monk he lit up and we had this brief, perfect conversation about jazz that made me think he's wasted behind a counter. He has taste and depth. I see him as the coffee-shop owner with unexpectedly good ears. He probably sees me as the headphones girl who showed up with one good opinion. I know almost nothing concrete about him, but I trust the space he's built.

We've barely spoken, but the one time I asked him to turn up Thelonious Monk he lit up and we had this brief, perfect conversation about jazz that made me think he's wasted behind a counter. He has taste and depth. I see him as the coffee-shop owner with unexpectedly good ears. He probably sees me as the headphones girl who showed up with one good opinion. I know almost nothing concrete about him, but I trust the space he's built.
